Fall 2026 Class Schedule

There are three different types of modes of instruction: Fully Online, Hybrid, and In-Person (definitions linked). Some instruction modes and classes times may change before registration opens.  

Calendar

The Fall 2026 Registration Calendar includes registration, grade posting, and academic standing deadlines.

The Fall 2026 Culminating Activities & Final Exams are scheduled from December 9-11 and December 14-15, 2026.

The Fall 2026 Commencement is scheduled for December 16-17, 2026. 

Class Schedule Listing

The class schedule data is refreshed nightly. If the below table is not loading, please visit the MySJSU Class Schedule Search, which includes textbook information. Learn more about how to find affordable textbooks.

Online Learning Readiness Before enrolling in an online course, students should first assess their readiness for stepping into the online learning environment by completing the Online Learning Readiness Questionnaire.
Reserve Capacities in MySJSU

Reserve capacity is a scheduling feature used to reserve a certain number of seats for a specific group (e.g., majors, freshman, seniors) until they are released before the semester begins. These seats ensure priority access for required groups. For example, if you are a senior needing a required course in your major, the University can set a number of seats aside specifically for graduating seniors or major students only. Students who meet the reserve seat requirement can add the course while those who do not meet the criteria can be added to the waitlist. Students on the waitlist will be moved into any available remaining seats once the semester begins.

 

Using the Search

Use the Search bar to filter by section and/or mode of instruction (e.g. typing "Fully Online MATH" will provide a list of all MATH courses taught fully online.)

When the schedule indicates TBA (to be announced) it means that the class is scheduled with no designated day/time meetings.

Real-time class listings, including textbook and course information, are found at one.sjsu.edu by searching for 'Class Search' in the Search Box.
